title: AccountRiskBaseline
description: The invisible contract between the AI agent, the database, and the UI. Every field Claude outputs must match this schema exactly — this is what n8n maps to Google Sheets columns and what Lovable reads for the dashboard.

account_name.description: Canonical enterprise client name. Normalize: 'ACME Corp', 'acme', 'Acme (ENT)' → 'Acme Corp'

arr.description: Annual Recurring Revenue in USD as integer. Normalize: '$500K' → 500000, '$1.2M' → 1200000

renewal_horizon_days.description: Days from today until contract renewal. Used in financial_risk_score formula.

financial_risk_score.description: Computed using formula: ((180 - renewal_horizon_days) / 180) * 100. If renewal_horizon_days > 180, use 0.2 as multiplier (score = 20). Round to 1 decimal place. Higher = more urgent.

revenue_at_risk.description: Computed: arr * (financial_risk_score / 100). Dollar amount at risk of churn. Round to nearest dollar.

technical_domain.description: Categorize based on symptoms. Rules: page load/UI/rendering issues → Frontend. Timeout/rate limit/endpoint errors → API. Broken charts/missing data/sync lag → Data Pipeline. Login/SSO/permissions → Authentication. Slow queries/data corruption → Database. Downtime/latency/scaling → Infrastructure. Third-party connectors/webhooks → Integrations.

bug_severity.description: P0: complete outage/blocker, P1: major feature broken, P2: degraded but workaround exists, P3: minor/cosmetic. Infer from language: 'can't log in', 'all users blocked' → P0. 'slow', 'sometimes fails' → P2.

days_issue_open.description: Number of days the issue has been reported. Extract from text: 'for 3 weeks' → 21, 'since last Tuesday' → calculate from context.

sentiment.description: Overall account sentiment based on CS language. 'FURIOUS', 'threatening to leave', 'escalating to CEO' → Critical. 'Really unhappy', 'disappointed' → Frustrated. 'Worried', 'asking for updates' → Concerned.

escalation_flag.description: true if executive involvement, legal/SLA threats, or explicit churn threat is mentioned.

bug_summary.description: One clear sentence describing the technical issue. Written for an engineering audience. Include: what is broken, how long, and user impact.

cs_owner.description: Name of the Customer Success Manager. Extract from message sender or body. null if not mentioned.

recommended_action.description: One actionable sentence for leadership. Must name a specific owner (Engineering, CS, VP level) and a timeframe.

engineering_ticket_ref.description: Jira/Linear ticket ID if mentioned in the message (e.g. 'ENG-4421'). null if not mentioned.

raw_feedback.description: The original unprocessed CS message — stored for audit trail. Never displayed in dashboard.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this record was last processed by the AI pipeline.

examples.bug_summary: SSO integration broken for 23 days since v2.4 deploy — entire Acme team locked out of platform.
examples.recommended_action: Assign dedicated engineer to ENG-4421 by EOD today with daily CS status update — escalate to VP Engineering if not resolved in 48h.
examples.raw_feedback: Just got off the phone with Acme — they are FURIOUS. SSO has been broken for 3 weeks now...

examples.bug_summary: Revenue analytics dashboard showing stale data for 9 days — charts not refreshing, client reporting on incorrect numbers.
examples.recommended_action: Data eng team to audit pipeline refresh job this week and communicate ETA to Globex by Thursday.
examples.raw_feedback: Globex really unhappy with broken charts, been 9 days now

_pm_notes.why_this_schema_matters: This is not just a data model. It is the contract that makes the entire system work. If Claude outputs a field name that doesn't match what n8n maps, or what Lovable expects, the pipeline silently breaks. Define this once, enforce it everywhere.
_pm_notes.financial_risk_score_formula: ((180 - renewal_horizon_days) / 180) * 100. Cap at 100. Floor at 20 (for renewals > 180 days out). This formula weights time-to-renewal as the dominant risk signal — a $100K account renewing in 14 days is more urgent than a $500K account renewing in 300 days.
_pm_notes.what_to_add_in_v2: Add churn_probability_ml (model-based score), competitor_mentioned (boolean), nps_last_score (integer), and open_ticket_count (integer) once you have more data sources.
